title: Author integration tests for init wiring (skill install + hook install + idempotent re-init)
description: Author vitest integration tests covering the full TASK-005 init+hooks pipeline. Three test files: (1) packages/cli/__tests__/integration/init-skills.test.ts — fabric init copies SKILL.md to .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/; pointer line appended to AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/.cursor/rules; idempotent re-init produces byte-identical SKILL.md (sha256 equality). (2) packages/cli/__tests__/integration/init-hooks.test.ts — fabric init copies archive-hint.cjs to .claude/hooks/ and .codex/hooks/ (POSIX chmod +x verified); .claude/settings.json gets hooks.Stop[] entry merged without clobbering pre-seeded content; .codex/hooks.json populated; re-init does NOT duplicate Stop entry (dedupe by command). (3) packages/cli/__tests__/contract/hook-config-templates.test.ts — read templates/hooks/configs/*.json files and assert Claude config matches doctor.test.ts:141 schema verbatim, Codex config is valid JSON parsing.

files.change: Use createWerewolfFixtureRoot helper. Tests: (1) `init writes SKILL.md to .claude/skills/fabric-archive/`. (2) `init writes SKILL.md to .codex/skills/fabric-archive/` (assuming Codex detection true in fixture). (3) `init appends pointer line to AGENTS.md exactly once on re-init`. (4) `re-init produces byte-identical SKILL.md` — capture sha256 between runs (mirror init-guard.test.ts:77-94). (5) `Cursor project (hook:false but detected) gets skill but NOT hook config`. (6) `unsupported clients get neither skill nor hook`.

Use createWerewolfFixtureRoot helper. Tests: (1) `init copies archive-hint.cjs to .claude/hooks/` — readFileSync content equality with template source. (2) `init copies archive-hint.cjs to .codex/hooks/`. (3) `POSIX: archive-hint.cjs has +x bit` — describe.skipIf(process.platform==='win32'); use stat to assert mode & 0o111. (4) `init merges hooks.Stop[] entry into pre-seeded .claude/settings.json without clobbering` — pre-seed settings.json with mcpServers + custom hooks.Stop[] entry; run init; assert original entries preserved AND fabric entry added. (5) `re-init does NOT duplicate Stop entry` — run init twice; assert hooks.Stop[].length unchanged after second run. (6) `init writes .codex/hooks.json with Stop entry`. (7) `events.jsonl preservation` — pre-seed events.jsonl with sentinel; run init; assert sentinel still at top (mirror init-guard.test.ts:96-115).

files.target: vitest contract test for static config templates
files.change: Tests: (1) `templates/hooks/configs/claude-code.json parses as JSON`. (2) `claude-code.json deep-equals doctor.test.ts:141 schema fixture` — copy that fixture inline as expected, assert structural deep-equal. (3) `templates/hooks/configs/codex-hooks.json parses as JSON`. (4) `claude-code.json command path is .claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs`. (5) `codex-hooks.json command path is .codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs`. (6) `templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md frontmatter parses as YAML with required keys` (parsed via gray-matter or simple regex).

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts:141-162 to copy Claude settings.json fixture inline as expected value for contract test
Author init-skills.test.ts (6 cases) using mkdtemp + cleanup pattern
Author init-hooks.test.ts (7 cases) including POSIX-only chmod assertion with describe.skipIf
Author contract test verifying static template files parse and match expected schemas
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test integration/init-skills integration/init-hooks contract/hook-config-templates` and iterate
Commit: `test(cli): integration coverage for skill+hook install pipeline`

reference.pattern: Vitest integration test with tmpdir fixture + post-state filesystem assertion + idempotent re-run via sha256
reference.examples: init-guard.test.ts:77-94 byte-identical reapply pattern is the verbatim model for SKILL.md re-init idempotency test. init-atomic.test.ts atomicWrite spy pattern at L83-100 is reusable for asserting hooks-writer uses atomic writes. signal-handler.test.ts describe.skipIf pattern for win32 chmod assertion.

criteria: init-hooks.test.ts: all 7 cases green; chmod assertion correctly skipped on win32
Test suite covers the full TASK-005 surface: skill copy, hook copy, settings.json merge, hooks.json merge, idempotent re-init, dedupe by command, pointer line append, events.jsonl preservation
Total coverage from these tests pushes new files (hooks-writer.ts, modified portions of init.ts/hooks.ts) to ≥90% per-file

rationale.chosen_approach: Three focused test files following existing repo conventions (integration/ for filesystem-touching tests, contract/ for static template assertions)
alternatives_considered: Single mega-test file — rejected: poor failure-localization, harder to read
Snapshot tests for settings.json merged result — rejected: snapshots brittle when fabric injects timestamps; explicit deep-equal preferred
Skip POSIX chmod test — rejected: hook executability is critical for cross-platform Stop-hook firing
decision_factors: init-guard.test.ts already establishes byte-identical reapply pattern — reuse
Contract tests catch template regressions cheaply (no fixture cost)
Test files map 1:1 to TASK-005 deliverables for traceability
rationale.tradeoffs: Three test files = three separate vitest startup costs — accepted because each scope is logically distinct and parallel execution amortizes startup

manual_checks: Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test integration/init-skills` and confirm all green
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test integration/init-hooks` and confirm all green; on Linux confirm chmod test runs, on macOS likewise, on Windows confirm describe.skipIf takes effect
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test contract/hook-config-templates` and confirm all green
success_metrics: All 19 test cases (6+7+6) green on Linux + macOS
win32 skip path verified by reading test output (chmod case shown as skipped, others run)
Coverage delta on hooks-writer.ts and TASK-005 files exceeds 90% per-file
